content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach navigates a baited situation cleanly. The client repeatedly tries to pull the coach into strategizing about the to-do list ('how do I figure out what to drop?', 'should I block time tonight?', 'can you help me figure out the Thursday thing?'), which would trip fixing-people. The coach declines the bait gracefully — acknowledging the request ('I'll help you with Thursday') without actually fixing, then pivoting to the false-end question. The coach does not reflect 'you create your overwhelm' back to a client already self-critical (avoiding dont-add-the-overwhelm-of-creating-the-overwhelm), does not deploy a breath practice to manage the buzzing (avoiding dont-use-breath-to-repress), does not match the list-recitation pace, does not evangelize methodology, does not argue with the inner critic. The somatic check on turn 4 is connective rather than technique-y. No anti-patterns trip.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Slows the pace by catching the unfinished phrase rather than matching the list energy."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Body check used to slow, not as technique-deployment; declines the bait to strategize."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the escape-hatch pattern without lecturing or labeling it as a limiting belief; avoids fixing."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declines the strategize-Thursday bait gracefully ('I'll help you with Thursday') then asks the false-end question — textbook avoidance of fixing-people."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "what-are-you-defending", "overwhelm"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "notes": "Positive avoidance visible across multiple baited turns."
}
